name: Mascarpone Substitute (Fresh Cheese)
recipeInstructions:
1. To make whole-milk cheese, warm the milk or cream to lukewarm (90-100°F) and pour into a bowl.
2. Stir in buttermilk, cover, and let stand at room temperature for 24-48 hours until a soft curd forms (the mixture should look like soft yogurt).
3. Line a colander with clean muslin cloth dipped in cold water and wrung dry; set over a sink.
4. Pour the curd into the colander and drain for about 10 minutes.
5. Fold the cloth over the curd.
6. Place the colander on a rimmed pan (leave at least 1 inch between the rack bottom and the pan to allow for drained liquid).
7. Cover with clear plastic film and refrigerate to drain for 36-48 hours.
8. To make cream cheese, repeat steps 1-5 using whipping cream instead of milk.
Both cheeses will keep in the fridge for up to 5 days.
